_meta.source_codes: ADMIN ONLY — NCT=NCERT, RDS=RD Sharma style, RSA=RS Aggarwal style, EXM=NCERT Exemplar, CBP=CBSE Past Paper, OLY=Olympiad pattern, ORI=Original

questions.question_text: The point (0, 0) on a graph is called:

solution_steps.working: The point where x-axis and y-axis intersect at (0,0) is the origin.
solution_steps.voice_text: The point where both axes meet, with coordinates 0 comma 0, is called the origin.

questions.question_text: The point (−3, 5) lies in which quadrant?

solution_steps.voice_text: In Quadrant II, x is negative and y is positive. Our point has negative x and positive y — so it is in Quadrant II.

questions.question_text: The x-coordinate of any point on the y-axis is always ____.

solution_steps.working: The y-axis is where x = 0 for all points.
solution_steps.voice_text: Every point on the y-axis has x coordinate equal to zero.

questions.question_text: Plot the points A(2, 3), B(−1, 4) and C(0, −2) on a coordinate plane and name the quadrant each lies in.
questions.diagram_description: Coordinate grid showing points A(2,3) in Quadrant I, B(-1,4) in Quadrant II, C(0,-2) on negative y-axis.
questions.correct_answer: A: Quadrant I, B: Quadrant II, C: y-axis (not in any quadrant)

solution_steps.working: x=2 positive, y=3 positive → Quadrant I
solution_steps.voice_text: A has both coordinates positive — it is in Quadrant I.

solution_steps.working: x=−1 negative, y=4 positive → Quadrant II
solution_steps.voice_text: B has negative x and positive y — it is in Quadrant II.

solution_steps.working: x=0 means it lies on the y-axis, not in any quadrant
solution_steps.voice_text: C has x equal to zero. It lies on the y-axis — not inside any quadrant.

questions.question_text: The temperature (°C) at different hours of a day is: 6am:18, 9am:22, 12pm:30, 3pm:34, 6pm:28. Between which two consecutive readings did the temperature rise the most?

solution_steps.voice_text: Find the difference between each consecutive reading.
The temperature rose by 8 degrees between 9am and 12pm — the largest rise.

questions.question_text: Does the point (3, 5) lie on the graph of y = 2x − 1?

solution_steps.voice_text: Substitute x equals 3 into the equation and check if y equals 5.

solution_steps.working: Calculated y = 5. Given y = 5. They match.
solution_steps.voice_text: Since the y value matches, the point does lie on the graph.

questions.question_text: Draw the graph of y = 3x by completing the table: x = 0, 1, 2, 3.
questions.diagram_description: Table: x=0,y=0 | x=1,y=3 | x=2,y=6 | x=3,y=9. Straight line through origin with positive slope.

solution_steps.voice_text: Substitute each value of x into y equals 3x to find the corresponding y values.

solution_steps.working: Points (0,0), (1,3), (2,6), (3,9) form a straight line through origin
solution_steps.voice_text: All four points lie on a straight line passing through the origin.

questions.question_text: A car travels at a constant speed of 60 km/h. Draw a distance-time graph for 4 hours and find: (i) distance after 2.5 hours, (ii) time taken to travel 180 km.
questions.diagram_description: Distance-time graph. x-axis: time in hours (0 to 4). y-axis: distance in km (0 to 240). Straight line from (0,0) to (4,240) with slope 60.

solution_steps.voice_text: At 60 km per hour, multiply time by 60 to get distance.
At 2.5 hours, distance equals 60 times 2.5 which is 150 km. Read it directly from the graph.
For 180 km, divide by speed. 180 divided by 60 equals 3 hours.

questions.question_text: Draw the graphs of y = x and y = x + 3 on the same axes. Do they intersect? What do you observe about the two lines?
questions.diagram_description: Two straight lines on same axes. y=x passes through origin with slope 1. y=x+3 is parallel, shifted 3 units up, crosses y-axis at (0,3). The lines are parallel — they never meet.

solution_steps.voice_text: For y equals x, every y value equals the x value.
For y equals x plus 3, add 3 to each x value.

solution_steps.working: Both lines have the same slope (1) but different y-intercepts. They are parallel.
solution_steps.voice_text: Both lines rise at the same rate. They are parallel and will never meet.

questions.question_text:
The following data shows the simple interest earned on ₹1000 at 10% per annum for different years. Draw a graph and use it to find: (i) interest after 3.5 years, (ii) number of years for interest to reach ₹750.

Years: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
Interest (₹): 100, 200, 300, 400, 500
questions.diagram_description: Line graph with x-axis as years (0-6) and y-axis as interest in rupees (0-600). Straight line from origin through points (1,100),(2,200),(3,300),(4,400),(5,500).

solution_steps.working: Interest increases by ₹100 per year — straight line graph
solution_steps.voice_text: The interest grows by ₹100 every year, giving a straight line graph.
At 3.5 years, the graph shows ₹350. We can also calculate it directly.
For ₹750 interest, solve the formula. It takes 7.5 years. Read from the extended graph.
